provost.name: Kate Hausbeck Korgan (Acting Executive Vice President and Provost)

cio.name: Kivanc Oner (Vice President for Digital Transformation and CIO)

ai_strategy_lead.name: Alethea Inns
ai_strategy_lead.title: Instructional Technology Support Specialist; Co-Chair Faculty Senate AI Task Force; Co-Facilitator Generative AI Fellows; Chair OneIT AI Working Group

documents.title: Building on Our Momentum: UNLV Rebel AI Initiative Expands

ai_strategy.summary: UNLV's AI strategy, operationalized through the Rebel AI Initiative (expanded September 2025), centers on three pillars: preparing students for an AI-enabled world, empowering faculty innovation in teaching and research, and strengthening services and operations while keeping human judgment, ethics, and equity central. The initiative is backed by a values-driven AI framework, an AI Oversight Group for alignment with institutional goals, and an AI Ecosystem Committee for infrastructure, policy, training, and adoption.

key_provisions: Faculty set course-level AI policies with three tiers: not allowed, allowed with documentation, or welcomed as a supplementary resource
Students must acknowledge AI use and default to disclosing assistance when in doubt
No personal, private, or sensitive data should be entered into AI tools
Consent required before using AI to record or summarize meetings
Sample AI syllabus language provided for faculty to adopt or adapt
Existing academic integrity policies apply to AI misuse
ai_policy.summary: UNLV takes a permissive, faculty-discretion stance rather than a single campus-wide mandate. Existing academic integrity, data protection, and research integrity policies are interpreted to cover AI use, supplemented with best-practice guidance from Information Technology and sample syllabus language from the Office of the Provost.

bodies.name: AI Oversight Group
bodies.scope: Aligns AI use with UNLV institutional goals and champions ethical, equity-centered adoption across teaching, research, and operations.

bodies.name: AI Ecosystem Committee
bodies.scope: Drives infrastructure, policy, training, and adoption frameworks in support of the Rebel AI Initiative.

bodies.name: Faculty Senate AI Task Force
bodies.scope: Formed January 2024 to advance integration of AI across the campus community; issued final report in May 2024; co-chaired by Alethea Inns.

bodies.name: OneIT AI Working Group
bodies.scope: Coordinates enterprise AI tool evaluation, deployment, and support across UNLV IT; chaired by Alethea Inns.

bodies.name: NSHE Emerging Technologies Advisory Group (ETAG)
bodies.scope: System-level advisory group (charter adopted August 2025) on emerging technologies across Nevada System of Higher Education institutions including UNLV.

governance_structure.summary: UNLV has evolved from a grassroots Faculty Senate AI Task Force (2024) to a multi-layered governance structure in 2025 that pairs an AI Oversight Group (strategic alignment) with an AI Ecosystem Committee (execution on infrastructure, policy, and training). These are complemented by the OneIT AI Working Group and the NSHE system-level ETAG.

initiatives.title: Rebel AI Initiative expansion
initiatives.description: UNLV's president-led framework that consolidates the university's AI efforts around three goals: preparing students for an AI-enabled world, empowering faculty innovation in teaching and research, and strengthening services and operations. Announced as an expansion on September 24, 2025.
initiatives.significance: Positions UNLV as one of the first R1 universities to pair a named institutional AI initiative with a two-tier governance structure (Oversight Group plus Ecosystem Committee).

investment.description: Funding details undisclosed; the initiative builds on the prior year's Generative AI Fellows stipends, AI Literacy Badge development, and faculty development programming.

collaborations.description: Coordinated with NSHE's Emerging Technologies Advisory Group to share resources across system institutions.

initiatives.policy_implications: Signals that Nevada's flagship R1 treats AI strategy as a president-level priority requiring sustained governance, not a one-off task force.

initiatives.title: Generative AI Fellows Program
initiatives.description: A one-year program run by the Provost's Office and UNLV IT that gives academic and administrative faculty stipends to train on generative AI, redesign assessments, develop clinical and creative simulations, build ethical literacy, and serve as campus peer resources. More than 200 faculty and staff across all UNLV colleges participated in the prior year.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates a scalable, stipend-backed model for faculty upskilling that has reached all academic units at a large public R1.

investment.funding_source: Office of the Provost and UNLV IT
investment.description: Stipends paid to fellows for a one-year commitment to training, workshops, and educational-material development.

collaborations.description: Internal cross-unit collaboration between the Provost's Office and UNLV IT.

initiatives.policy_implications: Empowers individual faculty to set course-level AI policy while investing in the capacity to do so responsibly.

initiatives.title: AI Research Hub (AiR Hub) at the International Gaming Institute
initiatives.description: UNLV's International Gaming Institute launched the AiR Hub on May 20, 2025 as a collaborative research ecosystem focused on AI's impacts, risks, and opportunities in the gambling industry. Founding members include Action Gaming, Axes.ai, Evoke Plc, Gaming Analytics, Kindbridge Behavioral Health, Playtech Plc, ROGA, Walker Digital Table Systems, and later Aristocrat.
initiatives.significance: First industry-academia AI research hub focused specifically on the global gaming sector, giving UNLV a national and international niche in responsible AI for a high-stakes regulated industry.

investment.description: Operational funding provided by industry founding members; specific amounts undisclosed.

collaborations.description: Founding member industry advisory panel funds and shapes the research agenda.
AiR Hub recently published research commissioned by the Massachusetts Gaming Commission on AI adoption in gambling.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates a template for domain-specific, industry-funded, ethics-forward AI research that other universities could replicate for regulated industries such as healthcare or finance.

initiatives.title: Enterprise rollout of Google Gemini to all faculty, staff, and students
initiatives.description: UNLV IT enabled Google Gemini for all UNLVMail and Rebelmail accounts on January 31, 2025 after Google added enterprise privacy protections. Conversations remain private and user data is not used to train the model. NotebookLM is included in the paid Gemini Pro tier.
initiatives.significance: One of the earliest large-scale R1 deployments giving every user — students included — a generative AI assistant with enterprise data-protection guarantees.

investment.funding_source: UNLV institutional / Google Workspace for Education
investment.description: Enterprise licensing integrated into UNLV's Google Workspace; specific cost not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Enterprise partnership through Google Workspace for Education.

initiatives.policy_implications: Enterprise deployment with data-protection commitments addresses FERPA and data-handling concerns that have slowed other institutions.

initiatives.title: AI Literacy Badge and EGG 116X introductory AI course
initiatives.description: UNLV launched a student-facing AI Literacy Badge with modules on how learning works, AI fundamentals, ethical use, citation practices, and a personal AI-use plan, paired with EGG 116X, an introductory AI course open to students across disciplines. A companion instructor guide helps faculty integrate the badge into their courses.
initiatives.significance: Combines curricular (credit-bearing course) and co-curricular (badge) pathways so every UNLV student can graduate with baseline AI literacy.

investment.description: Curriculum development and badge production funded through existing academic and IT budgets.

collaborations.description: Cross-unit collaboration across UNLV Libraries, Career Services, and Engineering.

initiatives.policy_implications: Raises the floor of AI literacy without mandating a single tool or stance.

initiatives.title: Minor in Data Science and Artificial Intelligence
initiatives.description: UNLV offers an undergraduate Minor in Data Science and AI that trains students in Python programming, data collection and analysis, machine learning, and AI applications. It is designed to complement majors across the university.
initiatives.significance: Provides a cross-disciplinary on-ramp to AI/ML skills for non-CS majors at an R1 serving a heavily Hispanic and first-generation student population.

investment.description: Standard academic minor operated by the Howard R. Hughes College of Engineering.

initiatives.policy_implications: Broadens AI degree-program access beyond computer-science majors.

initiatives.title: Data Science and AI Certificate and Bootcamps (UNLV Continuing Education + Institute of Data)
initiatives.description: UNLV Lifelong Learning partners with the Institute of Data to offer a 24-week Data Science & AI program with 400+ training and project hours, plus an AI Automation certificate and an AI Prompting certificate. These are online, non-credit, workforce-focused credentials with a 93% job-outcome success rate within 180 days.
initiatives.significance: Extends AI workforce training to adult learners and career switchers beyond the traditional degree pipeline.

investment.description: Fee-based non-credit programs delivered with external provider.

collaborations.description: External bootcamp providers delivering content under UNLV brand.

initiatives.policy_implications: Builds regional AI talent pipeline aligned with Nevada's Mountain-West workforce needs.

initiatives.title: Rebel Success Hub predictive student-success platform
initiatives.description: UNLV's Salesforce-based Rebel Success Hub provides advisors, faculty, and leaders with case management, referrals, early-warning alerts, and predictive analytics to identify at-risk students. A parallel UNLV research project extends this by combining LMS engagement metrics with motivational-survey responses for multimodal machine-learning prediction.
initiatives.significance: Connects enterprise student-success tooling with institutional research, modeling a feedback loop between vendor platform and campus data science.

investment.description: Salesforce-based platform integrated with UNLV student data.

collaborations.description: Underlying Rebel Success Hub platform runs on Salesforce.

tools_and_vendors.name: Rebel Success Hub
tools_and_vendors.role: Student-success case management and predictive analytics

initiatives.policy_implications: Operationalizes predictive analytics for advising within existing student-privacy policies.

initiatives.title: CyberCorps Scholarship for Service with AI/ML focus
initiatives.description: UNLV received a $3.1 million, five-year NSF CyberCorps Scholarship for Service (SFS) grant in 2024 to cultivate cybersecurity experts skilled in artificial intelligence and machine learning for federal and local government service.
initiatives.significance: Places UNLV in the small cohort of NSF-funded SFS institutions training AI-fluent cyber workforce for public-sector service.

investment.description: NSF CyberCorps Scholarship for Service award.

collaborations.description: Scholarship recipients repay via service in federal, state, or local cyber roles.

initiatives.policy_implications: Aligns federal cyber workforce policy with AI-skilled graduate production at a public R1.

initiatives.title: Nevada Institute of Cybersecurity (NIC)
initiatives.description: UNLV launched the Nevada Institute of Cybersecurity after NSHE approval on March 6, 2026. The interdisciplinary institute focuses research on AI-driven threat detection, IoT security, critical infrastructure protection, and secure data management, and coordinates workforce development through cyber competitions, a cyber range, and certification training.
initiatives.significance: Institutionalizes AI + cybersecurity as a strategic research and workforce pillar for Nevada at a time when state agencies face rising AI-enabled threats.

investment.description: Combination of institutional, system, and state funding; specific budget undisclosed.

collaborations.description: Student-led clinic serving Nevada small businesses.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates a named state-level institute connecting AI research, cyber risk, and public-sector service.

initiatives.title: 2026 President's Innovation Challenge: AI focus
initiatives.description: UNLV's 5th Annual President's Innovation Challenge (April 2026) is an explicit call for student-led AI ventures across business & hospitality, education & social science, and fine arts & entertainment, with judging criteria emphasizing commercialization path and impact.
initiatives.significance: Uses the flagship student-entrepreneurship competition to direct campus creativity at AI-native products and services aligned with the regional economy.

investment.description: Prize pool and mentoring support funded through the Office of the President; specific amounts vary by year.

initiatives.policy_implications: Frames AI not just as academic content but as a platform for student venture creation tied to Las Vegas's tourism, entertainment, and hospitality economy.

initiatives.title: Black Fire Innovation hub (Caesars Entertainment + UNLV)
initiatives.description: A 43,000-square-foot technology hub co-created by UNLV and Caesars Entertainment with mock hotel rooms, a casino floor, sportsbook, e-sports arena, and VR facilities used to prototype AI, robotics, and other next-generation hospitality/gaming technologies.
initiatives.significance: Provides a physical testbed that accelerates transfer of academic AI research into revenue-generating deployments in Nevada's dominant industry.

investment.description: Joint facility investment; specific figures not disclosed in public materials.

initiatives.policy_implications: Demonstrates a university-industry co-creation model for applied AI in regulated sectors.

initiatives.title: Playtech sustainable gambling AI partnership
initiatives.description: Multi-year collaboration between UNLV's International Gaming Institute and Playtech focused on using AI and machine learning to promote safer and more responsible gambling.
initiatives.significance: Channels commercial AI/ML research capacity toward harm-reduction outcomes, with global applicability to the gambling industry.

investment.description: Multi-year industry-funded research partnership; amount undisclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Sets a model for using AI to enforce rather than erode responsible-gambling safeguards.

initiatives.title: Accessibility-focused AI adoption (Gemini, Panopto, Ally)
initiatives.description: UNLV is pairing its digital-accessibility plan with AI-powered tools — Google Gemini, Panopto, and Ally — to expand automatic speech recognition, auto-generated captions, and accessible-content remediation, positioning compliance with ADA Title II digital accessibility rules effective April 24, 2026.
initiatives.significance: Ties AI adoption directly to federal accessibility compliance, making it a disability-inclusion lever rather than an optional productivity tool.
initiatives.policy_implications: Integrates AI with ADA Title II compliance obligations hitting state entities in 2026.

initiatives.title: Preparing for the AI Economy in the Mountain West 2025 report
initiatives.description: UNLV's Data Hub published a November 2025 report analyzing five Mountain West states (Arizona, Colorado, New Mexico, Nevada, Utah) on four dimensions: business AI adoption, AI job density, AI degree production, and federal small-business tech investment relative to GDP.
initiatives.significance: Establishes UNLV as a thought leader on regional AI-economy readiness and provides baseline metrics for state policymakers.

collaborations.description: Used Brainly's dataset on state AI readiness.

initiatives.policy_implications: Provides evidence base for state AI-economy policy discussions in the Mountain West.

research_centers.name: AI Research Hub (AiR Hub) at the International Gaming Institute
research_centers.focus: AI in gambling: operator adoption, regulatory frameworks, responsible-gambling AI, and industry benchmarking.

research_centers.name: Nevada Institute of Cybersecurity (NIC)
research_centers.focus: AI-driven threat detection, IoT security, critical infrastructure protection, and cyber workforce development.

research_centers.name: National Supercomputing Institute (NSCEE)
research_centers.focus: HPC and research computing supporting AI, biology, medicine, and engineering.

research_centers.name: Black Fire Innovation (with Caesars Entertainment)
research_centers.focus: AI, robotics, VR, and gaming/hospitality technology testbed.

research_centers.name: International Gaming Institute
research_centers.focus: Parent institute for the AiR Hub; research on gaming, hospitality, and regulated-industry AI.

research_centers.name: Nevada Institute of Personalized Medicine
research_centers.focus: Precision medicine research; applications of data science and ML to genetic and clinical data.

ai_academic_programs.name: Minor in Data Science and Artificial Intelligence
Data Science and AI Certificate (Lifelong Learning)
Master of Science in Computer Science (AI concentration)

enterprise_platforms.product: Rebel Success Hub (student-success platform with predictive analytics)

computing_infrastructure.summary: UNLV's National Supercomputing Institute operates Cherry Creek (housed at the Switch SUPERNAP data center) and a successor Cherry Creek II system, providing HPC capacity for AI, biology, medicine, and engineering research. A 2024 collaboration with Altair expanded supercomputing capabilities. Specific GPU counts are not publicly disclosed.

investments_and_gifts.title: NSF CyberCorps Scholarship for Service
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Train AI/ML-skilled cybersecurity professionals for federal and local government service over five years.

investments_and_gifts.title: NIH Health Research Network (Mountain West) renewal
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Lead a 13-university, seven-state health research network; enables data-intensive and ML-supported clinical research.

investments_and_gifts.title: NSHE / multi-state WildWEST NSF grant (UNLV subrecipient)
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Build technology workforce for field science across the West; includes UNLV alongside UNR and DRI.

investments_and_gifts.title: AiR Hub founding-member industry funding
investments_and_gifts.source: Action Gaming, Axes.ai, Evoke Plc, Gaming Analytics, Kindbridge, Playtech, ROGA, Walker Digital Table Systems, Aristocrat
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Ongoing operational support for AI research in the gambling sector.

industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: Co-created Black Fire Innovation hub for hospitality, gaming, and AI testbed research.
Multi-year responsible-gambling AI/ML collaboration with the International Gaming Institute.
Enterprise Gemini deployment for students, faculty, and staff with data-protection commitments.
Data Science & AI and cybersecurity bootcamps delivered under UNLV Continuing Education.
Expanded UNLV supercomputing capacity supporting AI research.
AiR Hub research commissioned on AI adoption across gambling companies and regulators.

industry_and_research_partnerships.partner: Nevada System of Higher Education (NSHE) / Emerging Technologies Advisory Group
industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: System-wide coordination on AI adoption, resources, and training (ETAG charter August 2025).

student_facing_ai.name: Google Gemini (enterprise)
student_facing_ai.scope: All students via Rebelmail accounts; enterprise privacy protections; available from January 2025.

student_facing_ai.name: Rebel Success Hub (Salesforce)
student_facing_ai.scope: Advisor-facing early-warning and referral system with predictive analytics across undergraduate population.

student_facing_ai.name: AI Literacy Badge + UNLV Libraries AI 101 Guide
student_facing_ai.scope: Self-paced AI literacy modules available to all students.

student_facing_ai.name: Career Services AI guidance
student_facing_ai.scope: Support and training for students using AI in career search and professional development.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: AI-powered digital accessibility (Ally, Panopto, Gemini)
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: UNLV pairs AI tools with ADA Title II compliance to expand captioning, alt-text, and accessible content for disabled users and English-as-additional-language learners.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Enterprise AI access for first-generation and Hispanic-serving student body
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Providing enterprise Gemini at no cost to every student closes access gaps for the 38.6% Hispanic, 51.7% first-generation Fall 2025 incoming class.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Equity-centered framing of the Rebel AI Initiative
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: AI Oversight Group charter explicitly champions 'ethical and equity-centered adoption' as one of its two core mandates.

regulatory_context.summary: Nevada's Executive Branch has a Policy on the Responsible and Ethical Use of AI (signed by the State CIO under Governor Lombardo). The 2025 legislative session produced AB 406 (restrictions on AI in mental/behavioral healthcare, signed June 5, 2025) and AB 73 (disclosure of AI in campaign materials); SB 128 (restrictions on AI-only insurance denials) was vetoed. At the system level, NSHE's Emerging Technologies Advisory Group (ETAG) charter was adopted August 2025 to coordinate AI across all NSHE institutions including UNLV.

controversies_and_litigation.summary: UNLV President Keith Whitfield abruptly resigned in March 2025 citing family health; interim leadership transition created uncertainty overlapping with AI strategy rollout. The prior year included the December 2023 campus shooting, which shifted institutional bandwidth to recovery.

narrative_summary: UNLV, Nevada's Hispanic-serving R1 flagship in Las Vegas with 33,706 students (Fall 2025), has moved quickly from grassroots AI experimentation to a structured institutional posture under the banner of the Rebel AI Initiative. The initiative's September 2025 expansion, signed by Interim President Chris Heavey and Acting Provost Kate Hausbeck Korgan, organizes work around three pillars — student readiness, faculty empowerment, and operational improvement — with governance split between an AI Oversight Group (strategy/ethics) and an AI Ecosystem Committee (infrastructure/policy/training). UNLV has not created a Chief AI Officer; day-to-day leadership flows through Vice President for Digital Transformation and CIO Kivanc Oner and AI strategy lead Alethea Inns. Enterprise adoption is notable: Google Gemini is live for every student, faculty, and staff account with enterprise data-protection guarantees (January 2025), complemented by paid ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini Pro+NotebookLM tiers for departments, and Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat at the basic tier. Faculty development has scaled to 200+ Generative AI Fellows across every college, supported by an AI Literacy Badge, the EGG 116X intro course, an undergraduate Minor in Data Science and AI, and bootcamp-style workforce programs. On the research side, the International Gaming Institute's AiR Hub (launched May 2025) and the Nevada Institute of Cybersecurity (approved March 2026) give UNLV two industry-facing AI research vehicles, each with distinctive niches — gambling and AI-driven cyber — plus a long-standing industry partnership with Caesars Entertainment at Black Fire Innovation. Policy remains permissive and faculty-led rather than prohibitive, consistent with NSHE's 'coalition of the willing' posture and Nevada's state-level AI policy under Governor Lombardo (including AB 406 limiting AI in mental-health care, June 2025). Gaps persist: investment totals are largely undisclosed, there is no named Chief AI Officer, and most initiatives are growing or scaling rather than fully transformative.

Names of AI Oversight Group and AI Ecosystem Committee members beyond Alethea Inns are not public.
UNLV has no named Chief AI Officer; ai_strategy_lead role is held by Alethea Inns alongside multiple other duties.
ChatGPT and Claude enterprise deployment sizes, license counts, and user counts are undisclosed.
Cherry Creek / NSCEE GPU counts and AI-specific compute capacity are not published.
Exact enrollment number used is Fall 2025 headcount (33,706); official Carnegie 2025 R1 confirmation from Carnegie itself not independently re-verified here (pulled from data/r1-institutions.json).
UNLV's AI policy has no explicit single 'adopted date'; guidance is distributed across multiple pages.
